jokes.text: Phone conversation escalating from 'I need $60,000' to 'I need $100,000, or I'm gonna lose both my houses!'
jokes.setup: Tracy desperately needs money for his house
jokes.punchline: The amount doubles when he remembers he has two houses
jokes.explanation: Perfect character comedy - Tracy's obliviousness to his own wealth while claiming poverty. The escalation feels natural and absurd.

jokes.text: Tracy escalates from needing $60,000 to $100,000 when asked 'which house?'
jokes.setup: Tracy needs money for his house
jokes.punchline: Reveals he has multiple houses by doubling the amount
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy character moment - escalation reveals his disconnect from normal financial reality

jokes.text: Tracy's money manager is Grizz, who lost everything in Worldcom
jokes.setup: Jack asks who Tracy's money manager is
jokes.punchline: It's Grizz, his bodyguard, who invested in Worldcom
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character integration - of course Tracy would have his bodyguard manage his money, and of course it would go poorly.

jokes.text: You must know Arsenio. Hall or Billingham?
jokes.setup: Jack mentions knowing someone named Arsenio
jokes.punchline: He asks if it's Hall or the made-up Billingham
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and misdirection. The audience expects Arsenio Hall, gets a fictional Billingham instead.

jokes.text: For the first time in your life, you'll be in a room full of women and you'll be the least crazy one.
jokes.setup: Jenna is going on The View
jokes.punchline: Liz points out this will be the first time Jenna is the sanest woman in the room
jokes.explanation: Sharp character observation that works on multiple levels - insulting Jenna while also insulting The View hosts.

jokes.text: The Rurr-Jurr has a limited release next week
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions her movie title
jokes.punchline: She can't pronounce 'The Rural Juror' clearly
jokes.explanation: Iconic 30 Rock moment - the title is literally unpronunceable, creating a running gag that becomes legendary.

jokes.text: You still don't know what the title is? No! No one does. It's gone on way too long to ask her about it.
jokes.setup: Someone asks about Jenna's movie title
jokes.punchline: Liz admits no one knows what it is and it's too late to ask
jokes.explanation: Relatable workplace humor - everyone's been in this situation where politeness prevents asking for clarification.

jokes.text: I love that we can work while we're on cocaine!
jokes.setup: Writers are working energetically
jokes.punchline: Casual admission of cocaine use as a work benefit
jokes.explanation: Dark humor typical of 30 Rock's writer's room culture, but feels somewhat obligatory rather than earned.

jokes.text: Could it be Roar Her, Gem Her? No, that doesn't make any sense. It's got to be Oral Germ Whore.
jokes.setup: Writers trying to decode the movie title
jokes.punchline: Each guess gets progressively more ridiculous and inappropriate
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation of mishearing - starts plausible, ends completely absurd. The logic of each guess is perfect.

jokes.text: What's up, flabby butt? You look weird today.
jokes.setup: Kenneth greets someone cheerfully
jokes.punchline: His greeting is an insult delivered with Southern politeness
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - his innocent delivery makes the insult even funnier.

jokes.text: You mean John Grisham. Oh, no. Kevin, John's brother.
jokes.setup: Discussion of author John Grisham
jokes.punchline: Jenna insists it's his fictional brother Kevin
jokes.explanation: Classic Jenna confidence in being wrong. The casual certainty about a fictional person is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Pete successfully says 'The Rural Juror' clearly
jokes.setup: Everyone else struggles with pronunciation
jokes.punchline: Pete nails it perfectly on first try
jokes.explanation: Good callback that subverts the established pattern

jokes.text: Did you know that before Kevin was a novelist, he worked at a recycling center?
jokes.setup: Jenna continues discussing fictional Kevin Grisham
jokes.punchline: She provides specific fake biographical details
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - Jenna doesn't just claim Kevin exists, she has a complete backstory. Perfect character work.

jokes.text: It's called Urban Fervor. Boy, these titles-- They really make you think.
jokes.setup: Jenna mentions the sequel title
jokes.punchline: Liz's sarcastic comment about the obvious rhyming pattern
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay setup with the rhyming titles, Liz's sarcasm lands well but isn't exceptional.

jokes.text: 'Urban Fervor' as sequel title
jokes.setup: Mention of sequel to The Rural Juror
jokes.punchline: Title that rhymes/parallels original in absurd way
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that extends the original gag perfectly

jokes.text: You remember that neighborhood festival where they killed a goat in the street? Yes! But we did have really good luck that year.
jokes.setup: Reminiscing about their old neighborhood's strange festival
jokes.punchline: Despite witnessing animal sacrifice, they had 'good luck'
jokes.explanation: Dark absurdist humor with perfect deadpan delivery. The juxtaposition of horror and nostalgia is expertly handled.

jokes.text: Oh, you mean Jesus? No-- Miguel from set design. He's over there.
jokes.setup: Jack describes someone who wanted everyone to love each other
jokes.punchline: Tracy assumes Jesus, but Jack means Miguel from set design
jokes.explanation: Perfect misdirection joke with excellent economy. The setup genuinely sounds like Jesus, making the mundane punchline perfect.

jokes.text: Eurek-o!
jokes.setup: Tracy has an idea
jokes.punchline: He mispronounces 'Eureka' as 'Eurek-o'
jokes.explanation: Simple character-based mispronunciation. Fits Tracy perfectly but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Although, I guess it's no surprise that Tony Hawk can't play blind.
jokes.setup: Discussing how bad Jenna's movie is
jokes.punchline: Reveals Tony Hawk was cast as a blind character
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd casting choice that perfectly exemplifies how terrible the movie is. Original and unexpected.

jokes.text: The Rural Juror. The Rrr-- Rural. The Ruh-- Rural. Eh.
jokes.setup: Pete tries to say the movie title
jokes.punchline: He struggles with pronunciation and gives up
jokes.explanation: Solid callback to the pronunciation running gag. Pete's defeat adds to the impossibility of saying the title.

jokes.text: You looked so beautiful. [awkward pause] The lighting was really neat.
jokes.setup: Liz tries to compliment Jenna's movie performance
jokes.punchline: Awkward pause before settling on complimenting the lighting instead
jokes.explanation: Perfect cringe comedy - the pause reveals Liz's struggle to find something positive to say about the actual performance.

jokes.text: Ethan and I both thought the programs were really easy to read.
jokes.setup: Liz continues trying to find compliments
jokes.punchline: She compliments something completely unrelated to the movie itself
jokes.explanation: Escalates the avoidance pattern - now she's complimenting the theater programs rather than the movie.

jokes.text: Tired of your sandwich making you angry? Then behold the Tracy Jordan Meat Machine!
jokes.setup: Standard infomercial opening about sandwiches
jokes.punchline: The premise that sandwiches make people angry is absurd
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist premise - why would sandwiches make you angry? Perfect setup for Tracy's ridiculous product.

jokes.text: Bologna, salami, boar-- Whatever! Into this sexy lady and she will melt them all together into one delicious food ball.
jokes.setup: Describing the meat machine functionality
jokes.punchline: Escalating from normal meats to 'boar' and calling the machine a 'sexy lady'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Tracy Jordan energy - random meat choices and inappropriately sexualizing kitchen equipment.

jokes.text: Never again will you have to suffer through the bread part of your sandwich.
jokes.setup: Tracy explains his meat machine concept
jokes.punchline: Treating bread as an unwanted burden rather than food
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd logic delivered with complete sincerity. The idea of bread as suffering is hilariously backwards.

jokes.text: Meat is the new bread! Uh-huh!
jokes.setup: Tracy's product demonstration
jokes.punchline: Nonsensical slogan delivered with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Perfectly memorable nonsense slogan. The enthusiastic delivery sells the absurdity completely.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Whoopi Goldberg. And you're Working Out With Whoopi.
jokes.setup: Jack mentions bad celebrity products
jokes.punchline: Cut to Whoopi Goldberg in workout gear
jokes.explanation: Standard celebrity product parody. Functional but not particularly inspired or memorable.

jokes.text: GE owns KitchenAll of Colorado, which owns JMI of Stanford, which owns Pokerfastlane.com, which owns Sheinhardt Wig Company, which owns NBC outright. NBC owns Winnipeg Iron Works which owns Ahp Chanagi Party Meats corporation of Pyongyang, North Korea.
jokes.setup: Jack explains corporate ownership structure
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd subsidiaries ending in North Korean party meats
jokes.explanation: Masterful escalation and satire of corporate complexity. Each company name gets more ridiculous, ending brilliantly with North Korea.

jokes.text: I've got two ears and a heart, don't I?
jokes.setup: Tracy asks if Jack likes Phil Collins
jokes.punchline: Jack's overly analytical response to a simple question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jack Donaghy line - turning a simple music preference into a logical equation. Excellent character writing.

jokes.text: The Rrr-Jrr
jokes.setup: TV host introduces Jenna's movie
jokes.punchline: Even professional TV host can't pronounce the title
jokes.explanation: Solid continuation of the pronunciation running gag. Shows the problem extends beyond the office.

jokes.text: Jenna plays a southern lawyer named Constance Justice.
jokes.setup: Liz reluctantly reveals movie details
jokes.punchline: The character name is absurdly on-the-nose
jokes.explanation: Classic bad movie character naming - 'Constance Justice' for a lawyer is perfectly heavy-handed.

jokes.text: Justice has been served. Good stuff.
jokes.setup: Character name Constance Justice
jokes.punchline: Frank's sarcastic appreciation of the obvious pun
jokes.explanation: Frank's deadpan appreciation of terrible wordplay is very in character.

jokes.text: The soundtrack was so moody.
jokes.setup: Liz tries to compliment Jenna's movie again
jokes.punchline: Another desperately vague compliment avoiding the actual content
jokes.explanation: Continues the pattern of Liz avoiding real feedback. Works as callback but not particularly original.

jokes.text: [Flashback montage of Liz's fake compliments]
jokes.setup: Jenna accuses Liz of giving condescending compliments
jokes.punchline: Cut to montage showing Jenna is absolutely right
jokes.explanation: Excellent visual callback that proves Jenna's point perfectly. The editing makes the pattern undeniable.

jokes.text: Well, that's not how I remember any of that.
jokes.setup: Liz just saw proof of her behavior
jokes.punchline: She immediately denies the evidence we just witnessed
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Liz's denial even when confronted with video evidence is very human and funny.

jokes.text: It's only 90 minutes. It's kind of a train wreck.
jokes.setup: Liz says the movie felt an hour too long
jokes.punchline: Jenna reveals it's only 90 minutes, making it even worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal that makes the movie even worse than we thought. The economy of the punchline is excellent.

jokes.text: What were they? Oaks?
jokes.setup: Liz mentions she wasn't kidding about the trees
jokes.punchline: She genuinely wants to know what kind of trees they were
jokes.explanation: Nice callback that shows Liz genuinely meant the tree compliment, making it even more pathetic.

jokes.text: I've always said humans need more animal blood. It keeps the spine straight.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman endorses the meat machine
jokes.punchline: His medical reasoning is completely insane
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman character moment - confidently delivering completely wrong medical advice with authority.

jokes.text: Some reds, yellows... Just got some purples in from Peru.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman offers the guys something
jokes.punchline: He's clearly dealing drugs, categorized by color and origin
jokes.explanation: Classic Dr. Spaceman moment - casually offering drugs like they're medical supplies. Perfect character consistency.

jokes.text: The Rrr Jrr is the true story of Rory Jerner, whose pure furor ensures a terrible murder--
jokes.setup: TV commercial trying to describe the movie plot
jokes.punchline: The description is as garbled and nonsensical as the title
jokes.explanation: Good extension of the pronunciation gag - now even the plot description is unintelligible.

jokes.text: Her last two Google searches were for singles yoga and scalp pain.
jokes.setup: Josh looked at Liz's computer while stealing the movie
jokes.punchline: Liz's search history reveals her sad personal life
jokes.explanation: Perfect character detail - these searches perfectly capture Liz's loneliness and stress. Hilariously specific.

jokes.text: I had to let Tony watch me pee to get that tape.
jokes.setup: Josh explains how he got into Liz's office
jokes.punchline: Reveals disturbing arrangement with security guard
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that feels somewhat gratuitous. Gets a reaction but doesn't feel particularly earned or clever.

jokes.text: I think the entire sketch is a train wreck.
jokes.setup: Jenna complains about wearing the Paris Hilton nose
jokes.punchline: She uses Liz's exact words about her movie
jokes.explanation: Smart callback that shows Jenna is deliberately using Liz's criticism against her. Good character work.

jokes.text: Teenmoviescene.com gave it five out of five iPods.
jokes.setup: Jenna defends her movie with positive reviews
jokes.punchline: The rating system is iPods instead of stars, showing how unserious the site is
jokes.explanation: Brilliant detail showing the questionable credibility of Jenna's positive review source. Very 2006-era specific.

jokes.text: [Liz accidentally hits someone in a costume] Oh, God, I'm sorry! I didn't know there was a person in there!
jokes.setup: Liz gestures dramatically during argument
jokes.punchline: She accidentally hits someone in a costume
jokes.explanation: Standard physical comedy beat. Works but isn't particularly inspired or memorable.

jokes.text: Pete, could you tell Jenna she smells like a stripper?
jokes.setup: Liz and Jenna are fighting through Pete
jokes.punchline: Liz's insult is petty and specific
jokes.explanation: Decent escalation of their petty fight, but the insult itself isn't particularly clever or original.

jokes.text: I did not have sexual relations with that woman.
jokes.setup: Liz wrote an impression Jenna can't do
jokes.punchline: Jenna attempts Bill Clinton impression badly
jokes.explanation: Shows Liz's petty revenge but the Clinton reference is fairly standard. Works for the story but not exceptional.

jokes.text: I can barely keep a lid on the feud going on between Kenneth and Dougie from props.
jokes.setup: Pete complains about managing conflicts
jokes.punchline: There's apparently a feud between innocent Kenneth and props guy
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd throwaway line. The idea of sweet Kenneth in a bitter feud with props is perfectly unexpected.

jokes.text: Hi, I'm Doctor Leo Spaceman. I'm a working physician with a degree from the Ho Chi Minh City School of Medicine.
jokes.setup: Dr. Spaceman introduces himself in the commercial
jokes.punchline: His medical degree is from a Vietnamese city, implying it's not legitimate
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dr. Spaceman character building - confidently stating credentials that immediately undermine his credibility.

jokes.text: We have no way of knowing because the powerful bread lobby keeps stopping my research!
jokes.setup: Asked if bread eats away at your brain
jokes.punchline: Claims the bread lobby is suppressing his research
jokes.explanation: Brilliant conspiracy theory humor. The idea of Big Bread suppressing medical research is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Actually, we all kind of liked it. It's not the best thing in the world, but it's solid and Jenna's really good in it.
jokes.setup: Liz expects the writers to agree the movie is bad
jokes.punchline: They actually liked it, undermining Liz's position
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that completely undercuts Liz's argument and leaves her isolated in her criticism.

jokes.text: I guess because I look weird, I can't be perceptive.
jokes.setup: Liz dismissing Frank's opinion
jokes.punchline: Frank's self-aware comment about his appearance
jokes.explanation: Frank's self-awareness about his appearance while making valid point about perception.

jokes.text: I guess because I look weird, I can't be perceptive.
jokes.setup: Liz dismisses Frank's opinion
jokes.punchline: Frank calls out prejudice against his appearance
jokes.explanation: Self-aware character moment that makes a valid point while being funny. Frank's insight into his own appearance is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Cheese and crackers, that smarts!
jokes.setup: Kenneth gets burned by the meat machine
jokes.punchline: His exclamation is adorably innocent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kenneth character moment - even in pain, he uses the most innocent exclamation possible.

jokes.text: She doesn't even need those glasses.
jokes.setup: Jenna calls Liz a fake
jokes.punchline: Her evidence is that Liz's glasses are fake
jokes.explanation: Perfect petty actress behavior - attacking someone's glasses as evidence of fakeness is hilariously shallow.

jokes.text: Not the Ukraine. I own some property on the Dnieper River. In Volyn? Closer to Cherkasy.
jokes.setup: Jack suggests selling the dangerous product in Ukraine
jokes.punchline: Tracy knows specific Ukrainian geography from owning property there
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character detail - Tracy casually knowing Ukrainian river geography is perfectly absurd and unexpected.

jokes.text: If it wasn't for me, you'd still be slutting it up for car dealership owners so they'd put you in their commercial.
jokes.setup: Liz and Jenna's fight escalates
jokes.punchline: Liz reveals Jenna's embarrassing past in local commercials
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of their fight with specific character backstory. Harsh but fits the heated argument.

jokes.text: This slut slept with your brother! Mitch? Yeah, and let me tell you something about Mitch. He is disgusting in bed.
jokes.setup: Jenna retaliates after being called a slut
jokes.punchline: She reveals intimate details about Liz's brother
jokes.explanation: Effective escalation that crosses personal boundaries. Works for the conflict but is more shocking than clever.

jokes.text: You know he's not right. He was in a really bad skiing accident!
jokes.setup: Jenna insults Liz's brother's bedroom performance
jokes.punchline: Liz defends him by revealing brain injury from skiing accident
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny defensive response that makes the situation even more uncomfortable and specific.

jokes.text: She came at me with that angry little badger face of hers-- There it is right now.
jokes.setup: Jack describes Liz's angry expression
jokes.punchline: Liz makes the exact face he's describing
jokes.explanation: Perfect visual comedy timing - describing the face while it's happening creates immediate physical comedy.

jokes.text: They've adapted The Rural Juror? I'm a huge Kevin Grisham fan.
jokes.setup: Jack hearing about Jenna's movie
jokes.punchline: Immediately buys into the Kevin Grisham fiction
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Jenna's lie has spread even to Jack.

jokes.text: They did warn me those diet pills were mood altering.
jokes.setup: Jenna realizes she's been acting erratically
jokes.punchline: Blames it on diet pills, explaining her behavior
jokes.explanation: Typical Jenna excuse-making that fits her character but isn't particularly original or memorable.

jokes.text: You have brown hair.
jokes.setup: Liz says she couldn't have been serious about acting
jokes.punchline: Jenna's reasoning is that brunettes can't be serious actresses
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd actress logic delivered with complete sincerity. The economy and character integration are flawless.

jokes.text: Yeah, but you couldn't have been serious about acting for a living. You have brown hair.
jokes.setup: Discussing Liz's acting dreams
jokes.punchline: Jenna's logic that brown hair disqualifies someone from acting
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Jenna logic - completely shallow reasoning delivered with total confidence.

jokes.text: This is boring. I'm bored now.
jokes.setup: Liz shares vulnerable feelings about her dreams
jokes.punchline: Jenna loses interest the moment it's not about her
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Jenna's complete narcissism. The timing and bluntness are expertly crafted.

jokes.text: Goldberg or Billingham?
jokes.setup: Tracy mentions giving the product to Whoopi
jokes.punchline: Jack asks the same Arsenio question but with Whoopi
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that applies the same naming pattern to a new celebrity. Works but isn't as fresh as the original.

jokes.text: Your father Werner was a burger server in suburban Santa Barbara... When he spurned your mother Verna for a curly-haired server named Roberta, did that hurt her?
jokes.setup: TV interview about someone's family
jokes.punchline: Excessive alliteration in the family backstory
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay with the excessive alliteration creating an absurd family drama. Well-crafted but somewhat disconnected from main characters.
